explanations.7: The 'target' field tells Docker which stage of a multi-stage build to use—in this case, it's building the 'development' stage, not production.
explanations.10: This syntax ${VARIABLE_NAME} pulls a value from your environment; if DOCKER_PYTHON_VERSION isn't set, the build will fail because there's no fallback.
explanations.12: The syntax ${VARIABLE:-default_value} means 'use DOCKER_APP_HOME if it's set, otherwise use /app'—the :- gives you a safe default.
explanations.21: This line sets which user (by ID) and group (by ID) the container process runs as; without it, containers often run as root, which is a security risk.
explanations.27: The period (.) means 'mount the current directory on your host machine' into the container at the path stored in DOCKER_APP_HOME, so your code changes appear instantly inside the container.
explanations.29: VSCode extensions are stored in a Docker volume so they persist across container restarts instead of being re-downloaded every time.
explanations.31: Bash history is stored in a volume so when you exit and re-enter the container, your command history is still there.
